steps.title: Separate actual and predicted
steps.content: The question assumes that participant P4 gave equal actual ratings to abstract and cubist paintings. The graph, however, concerns the model's predictions and their correlation with P4's reported ratings. For P4, the graph shows different correlations for abstract and cubist paintings, so the model did not treat the two painting styles in exactly the same way.

steps.title: Infer only what follows
steps.content: Choice B and Choice C choose a specific direction, claiming cubist paintings were predicted to give less or more aesthetic pleasure, but the data do not establish which style was predicted higher. Choice D restates the assumed actual ratings instead of describing the model's prediction. The unequal correlations support only the more limited conclusion that the model's predicted ratings for the two styles differed from one another. The answer is A.
